mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.9% is near the zero lower bound, constraining further monetary easing. As inflation (CPIAUCSL at 244.158) remains subdued, the Fed faces pressure to maintain accommodative policy. This sustained low-rate regime keeps long-term Treasury yields compressed. Any signal of persistent low FEDFUNDS over the next 20 trading days indicates continued monetary accommodation, preventing upward pressure on 10-year yields.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.9% near ZLB as of May 2017
regime_hint: post-crisis monetary accommodation with constrained policy space
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises more than 15 basis points over 20 trading days despite FEDFUNDS remaining below 1.5%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS at 146,063 thousand) is near full employment levels, and the unemployment rate (UNRATE at 4.4%) is below the estimated natural rate. Tight labor markets signal wage pressure and inflation acceleration, which forces the Fed to raise rates and steepens the yield curve. Rising long-term yields follow as markets price in future rate hikes driven by labor market tightness.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 4.5% or PAYEMS growth exceeds 200k month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.4% and PAYEMS at 146,063k as of April 2017
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls despite UNRATE < 4.5% and PAYEMS growth > 200k, or the interaction effect (joint signal) produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS at 474,948 million) and housing starts (HOUST at 1,215 thousand) reflect robust aggregate demand. Strong demand signals future inflation and economic growth, prompting expectations of Fed tightening. Market participants extend duration risk premiums and push 10-year yields higher in anticipation of rate increases and reduced monetary accommodation.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5% and HOUST > 1,200 thousand
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 474,948M and HOUST at 1,215k as of March 2017
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion across consumption and housing
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls more than 10 basis points over 20 trading days despite both RSAFS and HOUST meeting trigger thresholds, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO at 104.0998, indexed to 2017=100) is near trend and shows stable supply-side capacity. Stable supply conditions without inflationary pressure allow the Fed to maintain a patient stance on rate increases. This supply stability anchors long-term inflation expectations, keeping 10-year Treasury yields from rising sharply. Sustained INDPRO near trend signals no imminent supply shock that would force yield acceleration.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change remains between -0.5% and +0.5%
regime_hint: stable supply-side growth without inflationary bottlenecks
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises more than 25 basis points over 60 trading days despite INDPRO remaining within -0.5% to +0.5% monthly change band, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL (244.158) is below the Fed's 2% target in year-over-year terms. Low inflation removes urgency for aggressive Fed tightening. When inflation remains subdued, market participants reduce expectations for rapid rate increases, and long-term Treasury yields compress as the term premium shrinks. This mechanism operates continuously as long as inflation remains below 2.5% year-over-year.
regime_hint: low-inflation environment constraining Fed policy normalization
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises more than 20 basis points over 20 trading days despite CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth remaining below 2.5%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1 at 16,842.4 billion chained 2017 dollars) reflects solid economic growth. Strong GDP growth combined with tight labor markets (UNRATE 4.4%) creates a self-reinforcing cycle of demand, wage growth, and inflation expectations. This dual signal of growth and labor tightness forces the Fed to accelerate rate normalization, pushing 10-year Treasury yields higher as the market prices in a steeper rate path.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth > 2.0% and UNRATE < 4.6%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16,842.4B and UNRATE at 4.4% as of Q1 2017
regime_hint: synchronized growth and labor market tightness in late-cycle expansion
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls more than 30 basis points over 60 trading days despite both GDPC1 growth > 2.0% and UNRATE < 4.6%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate at 4.4% is at or below the Fed's estimated natural rate of unemployment (approximately 4.5-4.7%). When UNRATE crosses below the natural rate, the Fed interprets this as a signal of overheating labor markets and accelerates rate increases to prevent wage-driven inflation. Market participants anticipate this policy response and push 10-year Treasury yields higher in the 5-day window following a sub-4.5% UNRATE reading.
regime_hint: tight labor market with unemployment below natural rate
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls or remains flat over 5 trading days following UNRATE < 4.5% reading in more than 40% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST at 1,215 thousand) are elevated relative to long-run averages, signaling strong residential investment demand. Elevated housing construction increases demand for labor, materials, and credit, creating inflationary pressure. The Fed responds by raising rates to cool demand. Long-term Treasury yields rise as the market prices in a higher terminal rate and extended tightening cycle driven by housing-led demand strength.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1,200 thousand for two consecutive months
regime_hint: housing-led demand expansion with elevated construction activity
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls more than 15 basis points over 20 trading days despite HOUST > 1,200k sustained for two months, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
